---
title: "303-6: Dengue Single-Cell Multiomics Analysis"
subtitle: "GEX + ADT + VDJ Analysis of PBMC and Antigen-Sorted B Cells"
author: "Foo"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
    theme: flatly
    embed-resources: true
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 6
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
  cache: false
---

# Overview

This report addresses analytical questions from the **303-6 study**, which uses
10x Genomics single-cell multiomics (Gene Expression, CITE-seq antibody tags, and
V(D)J sequencing) to characterise immune responses in dengue virus (DENV) infection.

| Capture lanes | Data type | Questions |
|---|---|---|
| 1 – 5 | PBMC (whole blood mononuclear cells) | Q1, Q2, Q3 |
| 6 – 7 | Antigen-sorted B cells (Ag+ / Ag−) | Q4, Q5, Q6 |

---

## QC and demultiplex 7 subjects using bulk RNA-seq reference

## PBMC with Visc/CITE-seq (capture lanes 1-5)

- Use GEX and ADT to identify cell populations that can be detected for each of the 7 subjects separately  

- Use GEX to identify any DENV-infected cells, describing the cell types, and the number of infected cells/sample

- Use VDJ to demonstrate BCR sequences can be determined for B cells from each subject

## Ag pos and Ag neg sorted cells (capture lanes 6+7)
- For the Ag pos sort use feature barcodes to identify cells specific to each of the antigens DV1-4, by per-cell comparisons to HSA antigen, for each of the 7 subjects separately 

- Combine cells identified as binding any DV antigen from the Ag pos sort, use GEX to identify frequencies of B cell sub-populations, and compare these to their frequencies in the Ag neg sort for each of the 7 subjects separately

- Combine cells identified to bind any DV antigen from the Ag pos sort, use VDJ to identify the most frequent BCR sequences, and compare these to their frequencies in the Ag neg sort for each of the 7 subjects separately 

## With these we will identify next analysis steps with the collaborator, such as any DE comparisons to focus on

---

# Setup

## Load libraries

```{r}
#| label: setup-libs

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(Seurat)
  library(Matrix)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(patchwork)
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(scales)
  library(readr)
  library(stringr)
  library(purrr)
  library(ggrepel)      # label repulsion used in several plots
  library(tibble)
  library(knitr)
})

options(Seurat.object.assay.version = "v3")
```

## Output directories

```{r}
#| label: setup-dirs

for (d in c("PBMC_figures", "AG_figures", "VDJ_figures",
            "DENV_figures", "combined_figures")) {
  dir.create(d, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
}
```

## Helper functions

```{r}
#| label: setup-helpers

# ── File I/O ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

save_plot <- function(plot, path, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300) {
  ggsave(path, plot = plot, width = width, height = height,
         dpi = dpi, limitsize = FALSE)
  message("Saved: ", path)
}

vdj_b_files <- Sys.glob("./DATA/multi_config_[1-5]_filtered_contig_annotations.csv")


require_file <- function(path) {
  if (!file.exists(path))
    stop("Missing required file: ", path,
         "\nCheck getwd() and list.files(pattern = 'rds|tsv|csv')")
  invisible(path)
}

save_table <- function(x, path) {
  readr::write_csv(x, path)
  message("Saved: ", path)
}

# ── Seurat v4/v5-safe assay accessor ─────────────────────────────────────────
# Tries the 'slot' argument first (v4), then 'layer' (v5).
get_data <- function(obj, assay = NULL, slot = "data") {
  if (is.null(assay)) assay <- DefaultAssay(obj)
  if (!assay %in% names(obj@assays)) return(NULL)
  out <- tryCatch(
    GetAssayData(obj, assay = assay, slot  = slot),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (is.null(out))
    out <- tryCatch(
      GetAssayData(obj, assay = assay, layer = slot),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
  out
}

# ── Metadata helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

meta_with_cell <- function(obj) {
  obj@meta.data |> tibble::rownames_to_column("cell")
}

read_contig <- function(path) {
  config <- stringr::str_extract(path, "multi_config_[0-9]+")
  df     <- readr::read_csv(path, show_col_types = FALSE)
  df$config <- config
  df
}

pbmc_configs <- paste0("multi_config_", 1:5)

# Add a column to Seurat metadata from a named vector / lookup data.frame.
# 'key_col' is the matching column in 'lookup'; 'value_col' is what to pull.
add_meta_from_lookup <- function(obj, lookup, key_col, value_col,
                                  new_col = value_col) {
  idx <- match(colnames(obj), lookup[[key_col]])
  obj@meta.data[[new_col]] <- lookup[[value_col]][idx]
  obj
}

# ── ADT feature-name matching ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Uses exact word-boundary anchoring to avoid DV1 matching DV10, etc.
find_adt_features <- function(patterns, feature_names) {
  unique(unlist(lapply(patterns, function(p) {
    grep(p, feature_names, value = TRUE, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)
  })))
}

# ── VDJ barcode normalisation ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Strips the trailing GEM-group suffix (-1, -2, …) and prepends the config tag
# so barcodes match the Seurat cell names built during object creation.
normalise_vdj_barcode <- function(barcode, config_tag) {
  core <- sub("-[0-9]+$", "", barcode)
  paste0(config_tag, "_", core, "-1")
}
```

## Donor reference table

```{r}
#| label: donor-table

donor_info <- data.frame(
  subject   = c("14764","14765","14766","14767","14768","14769","14770"),
  timepoint = c("MD041 pre","MD012 d9","MD028 d9","MD032 d9",
                "MD023 d15","MD026 d15","CHI-17 control"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

knitr::kable(donor_info, caption = "Seven donors with timepoint labels")
```

## Load saved RDS objects

QC, doublet removal, clustering, and Monaco immune-cell annotation were
performed upstream and serialised as `.rds` files. Loading them here keeps the
report reproducible without re-running the full pipeline.

```{r}
#| label: load-rds

pbmc_filt <- readRDS(require_file(
  "PBMC_5lanes_singlets_filtered_Monaco_annotated.rds"))

ag_filt   <- readRDS(require_file(
  "AG_singlets_filtered_Monaco_DV_annotated.rds"))

# B-cell subset objects — loaded if already saved, otherwise built in Q5
pbmc_bcell_rds <- "PBMC_5lanes_Bcell_only_Monaco.rds"
ag_bcell_rds   <- "AG_Bcell_only_Monaco_demux.rds"

pbmc_bcell_obj <- if (file.exists(pbmc_bcell_rds)) readRDS(pbmc_bcell_rds) else NULL
ag_bcell_obj   <- if (file.exists(ag_bcell_rds))   readRDS(ag_bcell_rds)   else NULL

pbmc_merged <- readRDS("PBMC_5lanes_merged_with_demux.rds")

```

---

# Q0 · QC and Donor Demultiplexing {#q0}

**Goal:** Confirm that the upstream QC pipeline produced high-quality singlets
for all seven donors in both the PBMC (lanes 1–5) and antigen-sort (lanes 6–7)
captures. This section reports per-lane QC metrics and verifies that Monaco
annotation correctly assigned cells to their donor of origin.

Donor demultiplexing was performed upstream using genotype-based assignment methods (e.g., Vireo, demuxlet, or cellSNP-lite) with bulk RNA-seq reference data. The resulting Seurat object contains donor assignments stored in the subject metadata field, and only cells classified as Singletons were retained for downstream analysis.

To remove low-quality cells, PBMCs were filtered using the following criteria:

Assigned to a single donor (SNP_call == "Singleton")
Greater than 200 detected genes (nFeature_RNA > 200)
Less than 10% mitochondrial transcripts (percent_mito < 10)

The filtering step was implemented as:

pbmc_filt <- subset(
  pbmc_merged,
  subset =
    SNP_call == "Singleton" &
    nFeature_RNA > 200 &
    percent_mito < 10
)

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" appearance="minimal"}
## Background

Donor demultiplexing was performed upstream using a bulk RNA-seq reference with
**Vireo** (or an equivalent reference-based deconvolution tool such as
demuxlet / cellSNP-lite). The `.rds` objects loaded above already contain
singlet-only cells with a `subject` metadata column reflecting the assigned
donor. This section validates that assignment rather than re-running
demultiplexing.
  pbmc_merged,
  subset = SNP_call == "Singleton" &
    nFeature_RNA > 200 &
    percent_mito < 10
:::

## 0a — Per-lane cell counts after QC and doublet removal

## Donor Demultiplexing QC

![UpSet plot showing donor assignment and singlet recovery after genotype-based demultiplexing.](multi_config_1_combined_DemuxOnly_Singlets_upset_donor_assignment.png){fig-align="center" width="80%"}

```{r}
#| label: q0-qc-summary

qc_cols_of_interest <- c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent.mt",
                          "subject", "doublet_score", "doublet_class")
qc_cols_present <- intersect(qc_cols_of_interest,
                              colnames(pbmc_filt@meta.data))

qc_meta <- pbmc_filt@meta.data[, qc_cols_present, drop = FALSE] |>
  tibble::rownames_to_column("cell")

# Per-subject cell counts
subject_counts <- qc_meta |>
  dplyr::count(subject, name = "n_cells_after_QC") |>
  dplyr::left_join(donor_info, by = "subject")

knitr::kable(subject_counts,
             caption = "Cells retained per donor after QC + doublet removal (PBMC)")
readr::write_csv(subject_counts,
                 "PBMC_figures/Q0_cells_per_subject_after_QC.csv")
```

## 0b — QC metric distributions per subject

Violin plots of nFeature_RNA, nCount_RNA and mitochondrial percentage per
donor help flag any sample with unusual quality.

```{r}
#| label: q0-qc-violin
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 10

qc_metrics_available <- intersect(
  c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent_mito","percent_ribo","percent_hb","nCount_ADT","nFeature_ADT" ),
  colnames(pbmc_filt@meta.data)
)

if (length(qc_metrics_available) > 0) {
  p_q0_qc <- VlnPlot(
    pbmc_filt,
    features = qc_metrics_available,
    group.by = "subject",
    pt.size  = 0,
    ncol     = length(qc_metrics_available)
  ) &
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8))

 
}
 print(p_q0_qc)
  save_plot(p_q0_qc,
            "PBMC_figures/Q0_QC_metrics_per_subject_violin.png",
            width = 14, height = 10)
  
 v_cells_qc<- VlnPlot(
    pbmc_filt,
    features = c(
        "nFeature_RNA",
        "nCount_RNA",
        "percent_mito",
        "percent_hb"
    ),
    group.by = "monaco_main",
    pt.size = 0
)
 
  print( v_cells_qc) 
  
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" appearance="minimal"}
## Interpretation

Comparable distributions of nFeature_RNA and nCount_RNA across donors indicate
consistent library complexity. Elevated mitochondrial percentage can signal
poor-quality or dying cells; the upstream QC filter should have removed the
worst offenders. Donor-specific outliers here should be noted when interpreting
downstream results.
:::

## 0c — Batching confidence: UMAP coloured by Lane

```{r}
#| label: q0-demux-umap
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 6

p_q0_subject <- DimPlot(
  pbmc_filt,
  group.by = "Lane",
  label    = FALSE,
  pt.size  = 0.4
) + ggtitle("Q0: PBMC UMAP coloured by lane (demultiplexed)")

p_q0_subject
save_plot(p_q0_subject,
          "PBMC_figures/Q0_PBMC_UMAP_by_subject.png",
          width = 10, height = 6)
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" appearance="minimal"}
## Interpretation

If lanes are well mixed across all major clusters then this is
consistent with biology rather than technical batch effects.
:::

---

# Q1 · PBMC Cell Populations per Subject (GEX + ADT) {#q1}

**Goal:** Identify which immune cell types are present in each donor's PBMC
sample by combining transcriptome-based Monaco annotation with CITE-seq surface
protein measurements.

## 1a — UMAP coloured by Monaco annotation (all donors combined)

```{r}
#| label: q1-umap-combined
#| fig-width: 16
#| fig-height: 8

DefaultAssay(pbmc_filt) <- "RNA"

p_q1_umap_main <- DimPlot(
  pbmc_filt,
  group.by = "monaco_main",
  label    = TRUE,
  repel    = TRUE
) + ggtitle("Q1: PBMC populations — Monaco main") +
  theme(legend.text = element_text(size = 7))

p_q1_umap_fine <- DimPlot(
  pbmc_filt,
  group.by = "monaco_fine",
  label    = TRUE,
  repel    = TRUE
) + ggtitle("Q1: PBMC populations — Monaco fine") +
  theme(legend.text = element_text(size = 7))

p_q1_combined <- (p_q1_umap_main + p_q1_umap_fine) +
  patchwork::plot_layout(ncol = 2, guides = "collect")

p_q1_combined
save_plot(p_q1_combined,
          "PBMC_figures/Q1_PBMC_UMAP_Monaco_combined.png",
          width = 16, height = 8)
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" appearance="minimal"}
## Interpretation

This UMAP is a quality-control and orientation figure. Clean separation of
major populations (T cells, B cells, NK cells, monocytes, dendritic cells)
confirms that GEX clustering and Monaco reference annotation worked as expected.
The fine annotation panel shows additional immune subtypes within each broad
lineage.
:::

## 1b — UMAP split by subject

Each panel shows one donor; cells are coloured by Monaco main annotation.

```{r}
#| label: q1-umap-split
#| fig-width: 20
#| fig-height: 10

p_q1_umap_subject <- DimPlot(
  pbmc_filt,
  group.by = "monaco_main",
  split.by = "subject",
  label    = FALSE,
  pt.size  = 1,
  ncol     = 4
) + ggtitle("Q1: PBMC cell populations per subject")

p_q1_umap_subject
save_plot(p_q1_umap_subject,
          "PBMC_figures/Q1_PBMC_celltypes_per_subject_UMAP.png",
          width = 20, height = 10)
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" appearance="minimal"}
## Interpretation

This split UMAP shows how each donor contributes to the shared PBMC cell-state
map. Similar cluster structure across donors indicates the samples are broadly
comparable after preprocessing. Donor-specific enrichment in certain regions
reflects biological variation (infection/vaccination timepoint, immune
activation state) rather than technical artefact.
:::

## 1c — Stacked bar: cell-type proportion per subject

```{r}
#| label: q1-bar
#| fig-height: 6

celltype_freq <- pbmc_filt@meta.data |>
  dplyr::count(subject, monaco_fine) |>
  dplyr::group_by(subject) |>
  dplyr::mutate(percent = n / sum(n) * 100) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::left_join(donor_info, by = "subject") |>
  dplyr::mutate(subject_label = paste0(subject, "\n", timepoint))

p_q1_bar <- ggplot(celltype_freq,
                   aes(x = subject_label, y = percent, fill = monaco_fine)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", colour = "black", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Q1: PBMC cell-type composition per subject",
       x = NULL, y = "% of cells", fill = "Cell type") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
        legend.key.size = unit(0.4, "cm"),
        legend.text     = element_text(size = 7))

p_q1_bar
save_plot(p_q1_bar,
          "PBMC_figures/Q1_PBMC_celltype_composition_per_subject.png",
          width = 14, height = 6)
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" appearance="minimal"}
## Interpretation

Compositional differences across donors may reflect biology (e.g., elevated
monocyte frequency at day 9 of infection), differences in total cell recovery,
or donor-to-donor immune variation. This figure provides essential context for
later antigen-specific and BCR analyses, where B-cell abundance per donor
influences downstream signal.
:::

## 1d — ADT validation of GEX-defined cell types

Dot plots show surface protein expression (CITE-seq ADT) averaged per Monaco
cell type. Concordance between expected markers and their cognate cell types
(e.g. CD19 on B cells, CD14 on monocytes) validates GEX-based annotation.

```{r}
#| label: q1-adt
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 7

if ("ADT" %in% names(pbmc_filt@assays)) {

  DefaultAssay(pbmc_filt) <- "ADT"

  # Remove cells with missing or blank annotation before plotting
  pbmc_adt_plot <- subset(
    pbmc_filt,
    subset = !is.na(monaco_main) & monaco_main != ""
  )

  adt_marker_panel <- c(
    "Hu.CD3-UCHT1","Hu.CD4-RPA.T4","Hu.CD8","Hu.CD14-M5E2","Hu.CD16",
    "Hu.CD19","Hu.CD20-2H7","Hu.CD56","Hu.HLA.DR","Hu.CD11c",
    "Hu.CD123","Hu.CD27","Hu.IgD","Hu.IgM","Hu.CD38-HIT2"
  )

  adt_features_use <- intersect(adt_marker_panel,
                                 rownames(pbmc_adt_plot[["ADT"]]))

  if (length(adt_features_use) > 0) {

    # Enforce a sensible factor order for the y-axis
    pbmc_adt_plot$monaco_main <- factor(
      pbmc_adt_plot$monaco_main,
      levels = c("B cells","CD4+ T cells","CD8+ T cells","T cells",
                 "NK cells","Monocytes","Dendritic cells",
                 "Neutrophils","Basophils","Progenitors")
    )

    p_q1_adt_dot <- DotPlot(
      pbmc_adt_plot,
      assay    = "ADT",
      features = adt_features_use,
      group.by = "monaco_main",
      scale    = TRUE
    ) +
      coord_flip() +
      labs(title = "Q1d: ADT validation of GEX-defined PBMC cell populations",
           x = "ADT surface marker", y = "Monaco cell type") +
      theme_classic() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
            axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
            plot.title  = element_text(face = "bold"))

    print(p_q1_adt_dot)
    save_plot(p_q1_adt_dot,
              "PBMC_figures/Q1d_PBMC_ADT_validation_by_Monaco_celltype.png",
              width = 12, height = 7)
  } else {
    message("No expected ADT markers found in the object — check feature names.")
  }

  DefaultAssay(pbmc_filt) <- "RNA"
}
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" appearance="minimal"}
## Interpretation

Expected concordance: CD3/CD4/CD8 on T cells; CD14/CD16 on monocytes;
CD19/CD20 on B cells; CD56 on NK cells; HLA-DR on antigen-presenting cells.
Agreement between surface protein and transcript-based labels supports the
reliability of Monaco annotations used throughout this report.
:::

## 1e — Cell count table per subject × cell type

```{r}
#| label: q1-table

count_tbl <- pbmc_filt@meta.data |>
  dplyr::count(subject, monaco_fine) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from  = monaco_fine,
                     values_from = n,
                     values_fill = 0)

save_table(count_tbl,
           "PBMC_figures/Q1_PBMC_celltype_counts_per_subject.csv")

knitr::kable(count_tbl,
             caption = "Absolute cell counts: subject × Monaco cell type")
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" appearance="minimal"}
## Interpretation

Absolute counts complement the percentage barplot — a cell type may appear as
a modest fraction but still provide enough cells for meaningful downstream
analysis. Very low counts (< 10–20) in a specific cell type should temper
interpretation of donor-specific results for that lineage.
:::

---

# Q2 · DENV-Infected Cell Identification (GEX) {#q2}

**Goal:** Detect cells containing dengue viral RNA by querying reads mapped to
the DENV genome. This requires that the viral genome was appended to the
Cell Ranger reference at the alignment step.

::: {.callout-warning}
## Action required before running this section

Identify the exact DENV serotype / strain used for infection (or present in
patient samples) and confirm that the corresponding NCBI accession was included
in the Cell Ranger custom reference. The `denv_search_patterns` vector below
must match the feature names that Cell Ranger generated from that accession.

Common accessions: DENV-1 NC_001477, DENV-2 NC_001474, DENV-3 NC_001475,
DENV-4 NC_002640.
:::

```{r}
#| label: q2-search

DefaultAssay(pbmc_filt) <- "RNA"

# ── Edit these patterns to match your Cell Ranger feature names ───────────────
denv_search_patterns <- c(
  "^DENV", "^dengue", "^NC_001477", "^NC_001474",
  "^NC_001475", "^NC_002640", "^KM204118", "^KX830960"
)

denv_genes <- grep(
  paste(denv_search_patterns, collapse = "|"),
  rownames(pbmc_filt),
  value       = TRUE,
  ignore.case = TRUE
)

denv_detected <- length(denv_genes) > 0

message(
  if (denv_detected)
    paste("DENV genes found:", paste(denv_genes, collapse = ", "))
  else
    paste("No DENV genes matched. Patterns searched:",
          paste(denv_search_patterns, collapse = " | "),
          "\nAll feature names (first 20):",
          paste(head(rownames(pbmc_filt), 20), collapse = ", "))
)
```

```{r}
#| label: q2-score
#| eval: !expr denv_detected

pbmc_filt <- PercentageFeatureSet(
  pbmc_filt,
  features = denv_genes,
  col.name = "percent_DENV"
)
pbmc_filt$DENV_infected <- pbmc_filt$percent_DENV > 0

denv_summary <- pbmc_filt@meta.data |>
  dplyr::filter(DENV_infected) |>
  dplyr::count(subject, monaco_fine, name = "DENV_cells") |>
  dplyr::left_join(donor_info, by = "subject") |>
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(DENV_cells))

save_table(denv_summary,
           "DENV_figures/Q2_DENV_infected_cells_summary.csv")
knitr::kable(denv_summary,
             caption = "DENV+ cells by subject and cell type")
```


## VDJ-GEX Integration QC

```{r}
#| label: q3-vdj-gex-qc

library(dplyr)
library(tibble)

bcr_raw <- purrr::map_dfr(vdj_b_files, read_contig)

bcr_raw <- bcr_raw |>
  dplyr::filter(config %in% pbmc_configs) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    # Strip trailing GEM-group suffix, then prepend the config tag to match
    # the cell name format used when building the Seurat object.
    cell = normalise_vdj_barcode(barcode, config),
    mapped_to_pbmc = cell %in% colnames(pbmc_filt)
  )

# All unmapped VDJ contigs
na_contigs <- bcr_raw %>%
  filter(!mapped_to_pbmc)

# Unique unmapped cells
na_unique <- na_contigs %>%
  distinct(config, barcode, cell)

cat("Total unmapped VDJ contigs:", nrow(na_contigs), "\n")
cat("Unique unmapped VDJ cells:", nrow(na_unique), "\n")
```

### Why were these cells removed?

```{r}
#| label: q3-vdj-gex-filter-reasons

unmapped_meta <- pbmc_merged@meta.data[
  na_unique$cell[na_unique$cell %in% colnames(pbmc_merged)],
  ,
  drop = FALSE
] %>%
  rownames_to_column("cell") %>%
  as_tibble()

# SNP assignment
unmapped_meta %>%
  dplyr::count(SNP_call, sort = TRUE) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    caption = "SNP assignments for unmapped VDJ cells"
  )
```


::: {.callout-note collapse="true" appearance="minimal"}
## Interpretation

barcode	chain	contig
AAACCGTGTGGTTGGC-1	IGH	heavy chain
AAACCGTGTGGTTGGC-1	IGK	light chain
AAACCGTGTGGTTGGC-1	IGK	second light-chain contig
AAACCGTGTGGTTGGC-1	IGH	non-productive heavy
:::

### QC metrics of unmapped singleton cells

```{r}
#| label: q3-vdj-gex-singleton-qc

unmapped_singletons <- unmapped_meta %>%
  filter(SNP_call == "Singleton")

cat("Singleton cells not mapped to PBMC object:",
    nrow(unmapped_singletons), "\n")

unmapped_singletons %>%
  summarise(
    n_cells = n(),
    low_features = sum(nFeature_RNA <= 200, na.rm = TRUE),
    high_mito = sum(percent_mito >= 10, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_features = median(nFeature_RNA, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_counts = median(nCount_RNA, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_mito = median(percent_mito, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    caption = "QC metrics for unmapped singleton cells"
  )
```

### Combined filtering reasons

```{r}
#| label: q3-vdj-gex-filter-summary

unmapped_meta %>%
  mutate(
    fail_snp = SNP_call != "Singleton" | is.na(SNP_call),
    fail_features = nFeature_RNA <= 200,
    fail_mito = percent_mito >= 10
  ) %>%
  dplyr::count(
    fail_snp,
    fail_features,
    fail_mito,
    sort = TRUE
  ) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    caption = "Filtering combinations responsible for loss of VDJ-linked cells"
  )
```


```{r}
#| label: q2-barplot
#| eval: !expr denv_detected
#| fig-height: 5

denv_per_sample <- pbmc_filt@meta.data |>
  dplyr::count(subject, DENV_infected) |>
  dplyr::group_by(subject) |>
  dplyr::mutate(total = sum(n), pct = n / total * 100) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::filter(DENV_infected) |>
  dplyr::left_join(donor_info, by = "subject") |>
  dplyr::mutate(subject_label = paste0(subject, "\n", timepoint))

p_q2_bar <- ggplot(denv_per_sample,
                   aes(x = subject_label, y = n, fill = subject)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", colour = "black") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(n, "\n(", round(pct, 2), "%)")),
            vjust = -0.3, size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Q2: DENV-infected cells per subject",
       x = NULL, y = "Number of DENV+ cells") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

p_q2_bar
save_plot(p_q2_bar,
          "DENV_figures/Q2_DENV_infected_cells_per_subject.png",
          width = 8, height = 5)
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" appearance="minimal"}
## Interpretation

Higher counts may indicate stronger viral RNA detection, but must be
interpreted cautiously: ambient RNA carry-over and low-level cross-mapping can
produce spurious positives, especially when viral load is low. Compare DENV+
counts to the cell-type composition in Q1 to determine whether a particular
lineage is enriched.
:::

```{r}
#| label: q2-umap
#| eval: !expr denv_detected
#| fig-height: 6

p_q2_umap <- DimPlot(
  pbmc_filt,
  group.by = "DENV_infected",
  cols     = c("FALSE" = "grey85", "TRUE" = "firebrick"),
  order    = TRUE
) + ggtitle("Q2: DENV RNA-positive cells on PBMC UMAP") +
  labs(colour = "DENV+")

p_q2_umap
save_plot(p_q2_umap,
          "DENV_figures/Q2_DENV_infected_UMAP.png",
          width = 7, height = 6)
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" appearance="minimal"}
## Interpretation

Enrichment of DENV+ cells in a specific immune cluster (e.g., monocytes)
suggests lineage-preferential viral RNA detection. A scattered rare-positive
pattern across many clusters is less convincing and may require additional
validation (e.g., confirmation by ISH or bulk qRT-PCR).
:::

```{r}
#| label: q2-vln
#| eval: !expr (denv_detected && length(denv_genes) <= 6)
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 6

pbmc_denv_subset <- subset(pbmc_filt, subset = DENV_infected == TRUE)
if (ncol(pbmc_denv_subset) > 0) {
  p_q2_vln <- VlnPlot(
    pbmc_denv_subset,
    features = denv_genes,
    group.by = "monaco_fine",
    pt.size  = 0.5,
    ncol     = min(3, length(denv_genes))
  ) + ggtitle("Q2: DENV gene expression in infected cells by cell type")

  p_q2_vln
  save_plot(p_q2_vln,
            "DENV_figures/Q2_DENV_expression_in_infected_cells.png",
            width = 14, height = 6)
}
```

```{r}
#| label: q2-not-detected
#| eval: !expr isFALSE(denv_detected)

writeLines(
  c("No DENV genes detected in GEX matrix.",
    "Possible reasons:",
    "1. The DENV genome was not appended to the Cell Ranger reference.",
    "2. Viral load was below the scRNA-seq detection threshold.",
    "3. Feature names differ — inspect rownames(pbmc_filt) for any viral hits.",
    paste("Patterns searched:",
          paste(denv_search_patterns, collapse = " | "))),
  "DENV_figures/Q2_DENV_not_detected_notes.txt"
)
message("No DENV genes found — see DENV_figures/Q2_DENV_not_detected_notes.txt")
```

---

# Q3 · BCR Sequences per Subject (VDJ) {#q3}

**Goal:** Use VDJ to demonstrate BCR sequences can be determined for B cells from each subject

## Read and merge PBMC VDJ contig files (lanes 1–5)

```{r}
#| label: q3-load

# Adjust the glob pattern to match your directory structure
vdj_b_files <- Sys.glob("./DATA/multi_config_[1-5]_filtered_contig_annotations.csv")

# Fallback: search recursively
if (length(vdj_b_files) == 0)
  vdj_b_files <- Sys.glob("./VDJ/*config_[1-5]*/filtered_contig_annotations.csv")

message("Found VDJ B files (", length(vdj_b_files), "):\n  ",
        paste(vdj_b_files, collapse = "\n  "))

# Helper: read one contig file and tag with its config number
read_contig <- function(path) {
  config <- stringr::str_extract(path, "multi_config_[0-9]+")
  df     <- readr::read_csv(path, show_col_types = FALSE)
  df$config <- config
  df
}

#bcr_raw <- purrr::map_dfr(vdj_b_files, read_contig)
message("Total BCR contigs loaded: ", nrow(bcr_raw))
```

## Standardise barcodes and annotate with Seurat metadata

```{r}
#| label: q3-annotate

# Only keep PBMC configs 1–5
pbmc_configs <- paste0("multi_config_", 1:5)

bcr_raw <- bcr_raw |>
  dplyr::filter(config %in% pbmc_configs) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    # Strip trailing GEM-group suffix, then prepend the config tag to match
    # the cell name format used when building the Seurat object.
    cell = normalise_vdj_barcode(barcode, config),
    mapped_to_pbmc = cell %in% colnames(pbmc_filt)
  )

message("PBMC VDJ contig mapping rate:")
print(bcr_raw |> dplyr::count(config, mapped_to_pbmc))

# Pull a slim metadata table from the Seurat object
meta_slim <- pbmc_filt@meta.data |>
  dplyr::select(subject, monaco_main, monaco_fine, seurat_clusters) |>
  tibble::rownames_to_column("cell")

bcr_annotated <- bcr_raw |>
  dplyr::left_join(meta_slim, by = "cell")

message("Unmapped contigs after metadata join: ",
        sum(is.na(bcr_annotated$subject)), " / ", nrow(bcr_annotated))
```

## Filter: productive, full-length, paired heavy + light chain

```{r}
#| label: q3-filter

# Keep only productive, full-length contigs
bcr_productive <- bcr_annotated |>
  dplyr::filter(
    productive %in% c("True", TRUE, "true"),
    full_length %in% c("True", TRUE, "true")
  )

# Identify cells with at least one IGH AND one IGK/IGL
paired_cells <- bcr_productive |>
  dplyr::group_by(cell) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    has_heavy = any(chain == "IGH"),
    has_light = any(chain %in% c("IGK", "IGL")),
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::filter(has_heavy & has_light) |>
  dplyr::pull(cell)

bcr_paired <- bcr_productive |>
  dplyr::filter(cell %in% paired_cells)

message("Cells with paired productive BCR: ", length(unique(paired_cells)))
message("VDJ cells not in pbmc_filt: ",
        length(unique(bcr_paired$cell[is.na(bcr_paired$subject)])))
```

## 3a — BCR cell counts per subject

```{r}
#| label: q3-counts
#| fig-height: 5

bcr_per_subject <- bcr_paired |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(subject), chain == "IGH") |>
  dplyr::distinct(cell, subject) |>
  dplyr::count(subject, name = "BCR_cells") |>
  dplyr::left_join(donor_info, by = "subject")

save_table(bcr_per_subject,
           "VDJ_figures/Q3_BCR_cells_per_subject.csv")
knitr::kable(bcr_per_subject,
             caption = "Paired BCR-sequenced B cells per subject (PBMC, lanes 1–5)")

p_q3_count <- ggplot(
  bcr_per_subject,
  aes(x = paste0(subject, "\n", timepoint), y = BCR_cells, fill = subject)
) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", colour = "black") +
  geom_text(aes(label = BCR_cells), vjust = -0.3, size = 3.5) +
  labs(title = "Q3: BCR-sequenced B cells per subject (PBMC)",
       x = NULL, y = "Cells with paired BCR") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

p_q3_count
save_plot(p_q3_count,
          "VDJ_figures/Q3_BCR_cells_per_subject_barplot.png",
          width = 8, height = 5)
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" appearance="minimal"}
## Interpretation

Differences in paired BCR recovery across donors reflect B-cell abundance,
sequencing depth, and VDJ library quality. Cells that did not map back to
`pbmc_filt` (printed above) were excluded because they cannot be linked to a
GEX-validated cell type or donor identity.
:::

## 3b — Paired BCR cells on UMAP

```{r}
#| label: q3-umap
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6

pbmc_filt$BCR_paired <- ifelse(
  colnames(pbmc_filt) %in% unique(bcr_paired$cell),
  "Paired_BCR", "No_BCR"
)

p_q3_umap <- DimPlot(
  pbmc_filt,
  group.by = "BCR_paired",
  cols     = c("No_BCR" = "grey85", "Paired_BCR" = "red"),
  order    = TRUE
) + ggtitle("Q3b: Paired BCR-sequenced cells on PBMC UMAP")

p_q3_umap
save_plot(p_q3_umap,
          "VDJ_figures/Q3b_Paired_BCR_cells_UMAP.png",
          width = 8, height = 6)


pbmc_filt@meta.data %>%
   dplyr::filter(BCR_paired == "Paired_BCR") %>%
   dplyr::count(monaco_main) %>%
   dplyr::mutate(percent = round(100 * n / sum(n), 2))

```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" appearance="minimal"}
## Interpretation

Most paired BCR cells should localise to GEX-defined B-cell clusters. Cells
with paired BCR that fall outside B-cell regions may reflect imperfect
annotation or rare off-target captures and should be treated cautiously in
clonotype analysis.
:::



---

# Session Info

```{r}
#| label: session-info

writeLines(capture.output(sessionInfo()), "session_info.txt")
sessionInfo()
```
